title: Chapter 1: Basics, Pronouns & To Be
description: Master greetings, pronouns, and the verb 'är' (am/is/are) through deep repetition.

sentences.english: My name is Ana. (I am called Ana.)
No, my name is not Ana.
No, I am not a student.

sentences.swedish: Hej, jag heter Lars och jag är student.
sentences.english: Hello, my name is Lars and I am a student.

title: Chapter 2: Locations & Negations
description: Express where things and people are, practicing affirmative, question, and negative forms.

sentences.swedish: Nej, jag är inte på jobbet.
sentences.english: No, I am not at work.
No, the café is located there.
Yes, I am at home now.

sentences.swedish: Nej, nu är jag i skolan.
sentences.english: No, now I am at school.

title: Chapter 3: En/Ett Nouns & Adjective Agreement
description: Learn to match adjectives (stor/stort, ny/nytt) with common (en) and neuter (ett) nouns.

sentences.swedish: Nej, det är inte ett hus.
sentences.english: No, it is not a house.
No, the car is not new.

sentences.swedish: Ja, huset är nytt och stort.
sentences.english: Yes, the house is new and big.

title: Chapter 4: Possession & 'Att ha'
description: Learn to talk about what you have, blending previous nouns and adjectives.

sentences.swedish: Nej, han har inte en bok.
sentences.english: No, he does not have a book.
No, I do not have money.
I am hungry. (Contrast with 'I have' in other languages)

sentences.swedish: Jag har tid och jag är hungrig.
sentences.english: I have time and I am hungry.

sentences.swedish: Han har en stor bil och ett litet hus.
sentences.english: He has a big car and a small house.

title: Chapter 5: Present Tense Action Verbs
description: Learn to express actions and routines while repeating locations and descriptions.

sentences.english: No, I do not speak Swedish.
She drinks coffee at the café.

sentences.swedish: Jag pratar svenska och du pratar engelska.
sentences.english: I speak Swedish and you speak English.

sentences.swedish: Jag äter men jag dricker inte.
sentences.english: I eat but I do not drink.

title: Chapter 6: Reflexive Verbs & Routines
description: Learn verbs you do to yourself (washing, dressing) and master reflexive pronouns (mig, dig, sig).

sentences.english: No, I do not wash myself.
I get dressed. (I put clothes on myself.)

sentences.swedish: Nej, jag känner mig inte trött.
sentences.english: No, I do not feel tired.

sentences.swedish: Jag vaknar tidigt och jag klär på mig.
sentences.english: I wake up early and I get dressed.

sentences.swedish: Du vaknar sent och du tvättar dig.
sentences.english: You wake up late and you wash yourself.

title: Chapter 7: Expressing Likes
description: Learn to express preferences using 'gillar', 'tycker om', and 'älskar'.

sentences.english: No, I do not like coffee.

sentences.swedish: Nej, jag tycker inte om kaffe.
sentences.english: No, I do not like coffee.
No, I do not love tea.

sentences.swedish: Jag tycker om Sverige, men jag älskar kaffe.
sentences.english: I like Sweden, but I love coffee.

title: Chapter 8: The Future Tense
description: Express future plans using 'ska' (will/going to) with the infinitive.

sentences.english: I will / I am going to.
Yes, I am going to work.
No, I am not going to work.
Are we going to eat? / Shall we eat?
Yes, we are going to eat bread.
I am going to work today.
You are going to study tomorrow.
Are you going to work tomorrow?

sentences.swedish: Nej, jag ska inte jobba imorgon.
sentences.english: No, I will not work tomorrow.
I am going to buy a car.
Are you going to buy a house?
I will go to the school.
You will go to the job (work).
Is he going to go downtown?
Are we going to be at home?
Tomorrow I will study. (Note: time word first, verb second).

sentences.swedish: Jag ska studera idag, men imorgon ska jag jobba.
sentences.english: I will study today, but tomorrow I will work.

title: Chapter 9: The Past Tense (Preteritum)
description: Talk about what you did yesterday using simple past verbs.

sentences.english: No, I did not speak Swedish.
Yes, he worked at home yesterday.
No, I did not eat bread.
Did you go to the café?

sentences.swedish: Idag jobbar jag, men igår var jag hemma.
sentences.english: Today I work, but yesterday I was at home.

title: Chapter 10: The Present Perfect
description: Learn to talk about what you 'have done' using 'har' + the supine verb form.

sentences.swedish: Nej, jag har inte jobbat idag.
sentences.english: No, I have not worked today.
Yes, she has studied at home.

sentences.swedish: Nej, jag har inte ätit bröd.
sentences.english: No, I have not eaten bread.
I have gone to the school.
She has gone to the job.
Have they gone to the café?

sentences.swedish: Nej, jag har inte varit i centrum.
sentences.english: No, I have not been downtown.

sentences.swedish: Igår var jag hemma, men idag har jag varit på jobbet.
sentences.english: Yesterday I was at home, but today I have been at work.
